rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - T69FWL1

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TJGC6KT

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TYN2AER

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - TBUX8EZ

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

Resident said: washing machine button is not spinning
Additional Details: Issue arose 25APR2026 while washing a load of clothes. I was turning the dial as usual to select and lock in a load and as I pulled the dial back it came loose and stopped spinning. I attempted to push it back in to no avail. I am uncertain what might have caused it as I've had no issues prior.

Permission to Enter: Yes

Entry Notes: Two Black Cats free roam the house. They're friendly but a little skittish of new people. They'll keep to themselves and stay out of your way just please be wary of open doors.

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - TTRKIFK

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - TE0GCKCB

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TURHDZBB

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TPA1L7Q

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - TX3X8KL

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TW4ZM4BB

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TR0REGKB

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - TZJVCPJ

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - T8NYTDY

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - T45FYFBB

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.unit_address: 3401 Pacific Ave A105 Tacoma, WA 98418
rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TN4B8NAB

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

rows.service_request_description:
D - Maintenance - TV90F7L

Repairs as notated by Supervisor to Include:

Doors
* Evaluate doors to make sure they aren't damaged and also check if they need weather stripping or painting
* Verify that doors open and close easily and seal completely when closed

Appliances
* Verify that refrigerator is cooling
* Verify freezer temperature is below 16 degrees F
* Verify refrigerator door gasket seals
* Verify oven, broiler and each of the top burners on stove heat
* Verify vent hood is operational
* Verify disposal is functional and not blocked
* Run the dishwasher through a complete cycle and verify no water is leaking

Kitchen
* Verify kitchen faucet has no leaks near the spout or stem
* Verify no leaks in drain piping under the sink 
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn 
* Verify kitchen cabinets and drawers open and shut easily and that they stay shut
* Verify hot water comes out of the faucet


Bathroom
* Verify faucets don’t leak near spout or stem
* Verify no excessive chips or damage to countertops
* Check drain lines for weak areas that might leak later
* Verify caulking on the countertop is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify toilets flush, drain, and refill properly and don’t run
* Verify caulking around the tub/shower is not cracked or excessively worn
* Verify tub holds water and drains properly
* Verify doors on shower/tub operate properly
* Verify shower curtain rod is present and secured properly 
* Verify shower head sprays normally and doesn't leak near head or stem

HVAC
* Verify heating and cooling function properly

Interior
* Check for broken or cracked glass
* Verify windows have screens and are secured
* Verify windows open and close without excessive force
* Verify window locks operate properly
* Verify all lights and switches operate properly
* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TWG14WU

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

* Verify electric cover plates are not broken or missing

rows.service_request_description:
B -2 - Determine Source Unit - TJP05RK

Determine what units/s causing infestation and/or are non-compliant.

Pest Service Documentation Requirements for Vendors

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

1. Photographs (Required)

Provide clear, time-stamped photos of:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.

NAPCO phone consultation  - TF48B0R

Please text 1.847.763.4005 to set up an appointment for a phone consultation call with Jesse. She's in charge of accounts for NAPCO - North American Polymer Company, the supplier for the tub refinishing products we are trying to get an account with. 

She said she needs to do a consult with the tech that will be applying the coatings to ensure it is being used properly before they can activate an account for us. 
Some key points will be to confirm proper prep (no acetone etc to wipe down as it will react and cause lifting), proper equipment (they recommend/sell the Titan 105 HVLP setup), as well as safety precautions (ventilation, gear, etc) So it may be worth while to plug "tub re-coating process" into ChatGPT to have a cheat sheet before speaking with her.

When servicing any unit for pest-related issues, vendors must follow these documentation standards:

A. Pest Evidence

Live/dead bugs, nests, droppings, egg casings, entry points.

B. Contributing Housekeeping Conditions

Pots/pans or food left out

Exposed food or open pantry items

Accessible pet food

Greasy surfaces

Damp/wet areas

Excess clutter

Cardboard boxes (especially food boxes)

Trash or unsanitary conditions

Photos must clearly show conditions that may contribute to pest activity.

2. Tenant Statements

Document any tenant admissions regarding:

Knowledge of pests

Length of infestation

Contributing conditions

Delayed reporting

Include date, unit number, and exact statement (no interpretation).

3. Source Identification

If identifying a unit as a source, vendors must:

Provide specific supporting conditions

Include photographic evidence

Explain the reasoning (not just number of bugs observed)

4. Multiple Affected Units

When several units are impacted:

Evaluate the possibility of multiple contributing sources

Identify and document each contributing unit

Management may charge any apartment determined to be a contributor.
